---
title: "Assignment 2: Autocorrelation in Time-Series Regression"
author: "Omkar Navle | Roll No: A037 | SAP ID: 86062500050"
date: "2026-03-24"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

## Dataset: Walmart Sales Dataset | Source: Kaggle
Dataset link: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/yasserh/walmart-dataset

```{r load-libraries}
# LOAD LIBRARIES
library(readr)
library(lmtest)
library(nlme)
```

```{r load-data}
# LOAD DATA
# Update this path to where you saved the CSV file
data <- read_csv("Walmart.csv")

View(data)
str(data)
```

```{r summary-statistics}
# SUMMARY STATISTICS
summary(data)
```

```{r preprocess}
# DATE CONVERSION + SORT BY DATE (critical for time-series ordering)
data$Date <- as.Date(data$Date, format = "%d-%m-%Y")
data <- data[order(data$Date), ]

# HANDLE MISSING VALUES
data <- na.omit(data)
colSums(is.na(data))
```

```{r ols-model}
# OLS MODEL
model <- lm(Weekly_Sales ~ Temperature + Fuel_Price + CPI + Unemployment,
            data = data)

summary(model)
```

```{r plot-residuals-time}
# PLOT 1: RESIDUALS OVER TIME
plot(data$Date, residuals(model),
     type = "l", col = "blue", lwd = 2,
     main = "Residuals Over Time",
     xlab = "Time (Date)", ylab = "Residuals")

abline(h = 0, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
```

```{r plot-lag}
# PLOT 2: RESIDUAL LAG PLOT
e <- residuals(model)

plot(e[-1], e[-length(e)],
     col = "darkgreen", pch = 16,
     main = "Residual Lag Plot",
     xlab = "Lagged Residuals e(t-1)",
     ylab = "Residuals e(t)")

abline(lm(e[-1] ~ e[-length(e)]), col = "red", lwd = 2)
```

```{r plot-histogram}
# PLOT 3: HISTOGRAM OF RESIDUALS
hist(residuals(model),
     col    = "lightblue",
     border = "black",
     breaks = 30,
     main   = "Histogram of Residuals",
     xlab   = "Residuals")

abline(v = 0, col = "red", lwd = 2)
```

```{r durbin-watson}
# DURBIN-WATSON TEST
dwtest(model)
```

```{r gls-model}
# GLS MODEL (AR(1) correlation structure via nlme)
gls_model <- gls(Weekly_Sales ~ Temperature + Fuel_Price + CPI + Unemployment,
                 data        = data,
                 correlation = corAR1())

summary(gls_model)
```

```{r ols-gls-comparison}
# OLS vs GLS COMPARISON
ols_coef <- coef(summary(model))
gls_coef <- summary(gls_model)$tTable

comparison <- data.frame(
  Variable  = rownames(ols_coef),
  OLS_Coeff = ols_coef[, "Estimate"],
  GLS_Coeff = gls_coef[, "Value"])

comparison
```

```{r estimate-rho}
# ESTIMATE RHO FROM RESIDUALS
rho <- sum(e[-1] * e[-length(e)]) / sum(e[-length(e)]^2)
rho
```

```{r cochrane-orcutt}
# MANUAL COCHRANE-ORCUTT TRANSFORMATION
Y  <- data$Weekly_Sales
X1 <- data$Temperature
X2 <- data$Fuel_Price
X3 <- data$CPI
X4 <- data$Unemployment

# Quasi-differenced variables: Y*_t = Y_t - rho * Y_{t-1}
Y_star  <- Y[-1]  - rho * Y[-length(Y)]
X1_star <- X1[-1] - rho * X1[-length(X1)]
X2_star <- X2[-1] - rho * X2[-length(X2)]
X3_star <- X3[-1] - rho * X3[-length(X3)]
X4_star <- X4[-1] - rho * X4[-length(X4)]

# OLS on transformed model = Cochrane-Orcutt GLS
model_gls_manual <- lm(Y_star ~ X1_star + X2_star + X3_star + X4_star)

summary(model_gls_manual)
```

```{r refined-model}
# REFINED MODEL (Fuel_Price removed — consistently insignificant)
model_gls_manual_refined <- lm(Y_star ~ X1_star + X2_star + X4_star)

summary(model_gls_manual_refined)
```
